english: — raṅ-k'a (rang kha) Sch. = raṅ-bu? (rang bu?) — raṅ-k'óṅs (rang khongs) = raṅ-k'úl (rang khul) territory, district C. (?) — raṅ-°k'ós (rang 'khos) one's own worth, affairs, necessities Sch. — raṅ-grúb (rang grub) not made or produced by men, self-produced. — raṅ-dga-ba (rang dga' ba) free, independent, raṅ-dga-pa (rang dga' pa) an unmarried man Sch. — raṅ-rgyál (rang rgyal) 1. Stg.: = raṅ-saṅs-rgyás (rang sangs rgyas). 2. raṅ-rgyál-gyis °gro-ba (rang rgyal gyis 'gro ba) Sch.: to live after one's own option or pleasure (?) — raṅ-rgyú (rang rgyu) Sch.: 'die eigene Ursache, Selbstfolge' (?!) — raṅ-ṅó (rang ngo) one's own nature, śes-pa (shes pa) to know Mil. — raṅ-nyíd (rang nyid) himself, herself etc., one's self Mil., raṅ-nyíd gról-ba (rang nyid grol ba) to deliver one's self Thgy., bdud raṅ-nyíd (bdud rang nyid) the devil himself in his own person Tar. — raṅ-°t'ág (rang 'thag) mill, water-mill. — raṅ-mt'óṅ (rang mthong) pride, self-complacency, self-sufficiency Mil., Glr. — raṅ-dón (rang don) one's own affairs, one's own profit, raṅ-dón byéd-pa (rang don byed pa) to look to one's own advantage Do., raṅ-°dód (rang 'dod) selfishness, v. raṅ-rtsís (rang rtsis). — raṅ-snáṅ (rang snang) v. sub snáṅ-ba (snang ba); Sch. also: self-born. — raṅ-po (rang po) Cs. = po-raṅ (po rang) an unmarried man. — raṅ-bábs (rang babs) v. babs (babs). — raṅ-bu (rang bu) 1. Cs. single, alone, raṅ-bur (rang bur) adv. singly, alone, without a consort. 2. Cs.: a single life (?). 3. Schr.: one's own child. — raṅ-byúṅ (rang byung), raṅ-byóṅ (rang byon) self-born, having originated of itself, = raṅ-grúb (rang grub) frq. raṅ-dbaṅ (rang dbang) independence, liberty, raṅ-dbaṅ t'ób-pa (rang dbang thob pa) to become free Glr.; gnás-la raṅ-dbaṅ-méd (gnas la rang dbang med) they are not master of the place i.e. they are not free to choose the place Thgy., in the same sense, gar skye raṅ-dbaṅ-méd (gar skye rang dbang med) Mil.; *raṅ-wáṅ °júg-pa* (rang wang 'jug pa) to set free C.; raṅ-dbáṅ-càn (rang dbang can) free W. — raṅ-°bàr (rang 'bar) Cs.: 'musket', in W. it is only used for pistol; *raṅ-bár dug-rág* (rang bar dug rag) W. a revolver. — raṅ-mo (rang mo) Cs. = mo-raṅ (mo rang) an unmarried woman. — raṅ-rtsís (rang rtsis) the opinion which one has of one's self, raṅ-rtsís daṅ raṅ-°dód ma c'e źig (rang rtsis dang rang 'dod ma che zhig) think little of your own self! Mil. — raṅ-bźín (rang bzhin), natural disposition, state or constitution, nature, temper, raṅ-bźín-las gźán-du °gyúr-ba (rang bzhin las gzhan du 'gyur ba) to change one's natural constitution Wdṅ., °bab dé-ltar c'é-bai raṅ-bźín-gyis ('bab de ltar che ba'i rang bzhin gyis) as a natural consequence of so heavy a snow-fall Mil.; raṅ-bźín-gyis (rang bzhin gyis) of itself, by itself, from its very nature, naturally, spontaneously Dzl., in col. language, raṅ-bźín-nas (rang bzhin nas) id., also for self in the sense: I, he etc. without the aid and independently of others; °byúṅ-ba lṅai raṅ-bźín-can-gyi lus °di ('byung ba lnga'i rang bzhin can gyi lus 'di) this body participating of the nature of the five elements Wdṅ.; draṅ-poi raṅ-bźín-can-gyi p'yir (drang po'i rang bzhin can gyi phyir) for raṅ-bźín-can yín-pai p'yir (rang bzhin can yin pa'i phyir) Sbh. — *raṅ-źín ǰọ́-pa* (rang zhin jo pa) C. needless words, where it is a matter of course; also: talk without any serious intent; *de da raṅ-źín-la zér-c'e źig yod* (de da rang zhin la zer che zhig yod) W. that is nothing but talk. — raṅ-bzó (rang bzo) 1. Lex.: the right, proper form (of a word)? 2. self-determination, opp. to a punctilious adhering to tradition Mil. — raṅ-raṅ (rang rang) each... himself, each... his, her, its etc. (not reciprocally, as Sch. has it), raṅ-raṅ-gi k'rii °óg-tu sbas (rang rang gi khri'i 'og tu sbas) he buried each (idol) under its own seat Glr., raṅ-raṅ-gi leur gsal (rang rang gi le'ur gsal) each (subject) will be explained in its own chapter Lt. — raṅ-raṅ-lao (rang rang la'o) each (final consonant) has itself (joined), i.e. is doubled Gram. — raṅ-ré (rang re) 1. = raṅ-ráṅ (rang rang): raṅ-réi sna-t'ág raṅ-rés zuṅ (rang re'i sna thag rang res zung) each may lead himself, may be his own guide. 2. we, raṅ-réi sgo drúṅ-na (rang re'i sgo drung na) at our own door Mil., raṅ-ré-rnams (rang re rnams) we (the Lamas, opp. to the laymen) Mil. 3. polite way of addressing, for our you or the German 'Sie' Thgr.? — raṅ-śúgs-la (rang shugs la) of itself, spontaneously W. — raṅ-sa (rang sa), raṅ-so (rang so) one's own place, raṅ-sa °dzín-pa (rang sa 'dzin pa) to maintain one's place, one's station Mil., prob. like raṅ-mgo °t'ón-pa (rang mgo 'thon pa); raṅ-sar (rang sar), raṅ-sor (rang sor) 1. bźág-pa (bzhag pa) to put (a thing) in its place, fig. for: to leave undecided, to let the matter alone, ni f. Mil. 2. of itself, e.g. raṅ-sor źi (rang sor zhi) (a storm) abates of itself. — raṅ-saṅs-rgyas (rang sangs rgyas) i.e. a Buddha who has obtained his Buddhaship alone by his own exercises of penance, but who does not promote the welfare of other beings.

pf. raṅs (rangs), to rejoice, sems mí-raṅs-par (sems mi rangs par) discontented, yid-raṅ-ba (yid rang ba) or yi-raṅ-ba (yi rang ba) id., frq.; *dhé-la ga-ráṅ-dhág-te* (dhe la ga rang dhag te) highly pleased with it C.; yid ma raṅs-śiṅ mi mgú-bar gyúr-te (yid ma rangs shing mi mgu bar gyur te) being very much dissatisfied Stg.; ma-raṅ-bźin-du (ma rang bzhin du) unwillingly, reluctantly.
